howItWorks.intro: This halloween candy cost calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Total Pieces = Expected Kids x Pieces per Child x (1 + Buffer %)
Candy Cost = Total Pieces x Cost per Piece
Bags Needed = Total Pieces / Pieces per Bag
Cost per Trick-or-Treater = Candy Cost / Expected Kids
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates. Consult a professional for critical decisions.

faq.question: How much candy do I need for Halloween?
faq.answer: Plan for 2-3 pieces per child. In an average suburban neighborhood, expect 50-100 trick-or-treaters. Buy 20% extra as a buffer. Track your numbers each year to refine the estimate.

faq.question: What is the most cost-effective Halloween candy?
faq.answer: Bulk variety bags from warehouse stores offer the best per-piece value at $0.06-0.10 each. Name brand bags from grocery stores average $0.12-0.18 per piece. Buy after-season clearance candy for next year at 50-75% off.

faq.question: When is the best time to buy Halloween candy?
faq.answer: Buy candy in early-to-mid October for the best selection. Prices are lowest at warehouse stores. Avoid buying too early or you will eat it before Halloween. November 1st clearance sales offer 50-75% off.

faq.question: Should I give out full-size bars?
faq.answer: Full-size bars make you the neighborhood hero but cost 7-10x more than fun-size. A compromise is giving full-size bars to the first 20-30 kids and switching to fun-size after that.
